rationale: The cited paper is used in literature-review style passages to exemplify that process versus outcome simulation can have different effects in marketing contexts (especially product evaluation), and later as a brief exception note about antecedents of simulation. The citing paper does not rely on this specific article for its core hypotheses, design, measures, or analysis; its main theoretical development centers on duration framing and personal relevance with broader mental-simulation references. Because Zhao et al. (2011) is not given a distinct, paper-specific functional role beyond background positioning, the citation is best treated as a passing mention.
